item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 China recalculates Taiwan strategy as US-Iran crisis dominates global attention
item.description: Beijing is intensifying diplomatic pressure on Taiwan ahead of 2028 elections while simultaneously positioning itself as a global peacemaker amid the Gulf crisis. China has escalated tensions with Japan over Taiwan comments and is pushing the narrative that a Kuomintang victory would avert conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸✊ Veterans arrested at US Capitol during anti-war protest against Iran conflict
item.description: Dozens of veterans and military family members protesting the Iran war were arrested by US Capitol Police after occupying the Capitol building. The demonstration reflects growing domestic opposition to US military involvement in the Gulf.

item.title: 🇵🇸⛪ Hamas disarmament talks stalled as peace envoy admits negotiations difficult
item.description: Board of Peace envoy Nickolay Mladenov acknowledged that Hamas disarmament negotiations have stalled after the group ignored his ultimatum. He warned an agreement must be reached within two weeks at most.

item.title: ⚠️🇵🇰 US and Iran signal readiness for war as Pakistan ceasefire talks remain in limbo
item.description: The White House confirmed JD Vance is prepared to fly back to Islamabad for another round of talks, but Tehran has not confirmed participation. Both sides are warning they are ready for continued conflict if the ceasefire extension fails.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Considers Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Nears
item.description: Iran is evaluating participation in US peace talks in Pakistan following moves to end a US blockade of Iranian ports. The two-week ceasefire is set to expire, with uncertainty growing over the fate of negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Promises Better Nuclear Deal Than 2015 Agreement
item.description: President Trump said the new nuclear deal being negotiated with Iran will be superior to the 2015 international agreement, as his administration pushes for a resolution before the ceasefire expires.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Fitch Cuts Philippines Outlook to Negative on Energy Shock
item.description: Credit ratings agency Fitch revised the Philippines outlook to negative from stable, citing medium-term growth risks from disrupted public investment and high exposure to the global energy crisis.

item.title: ⛽ EU Has Five Months of Jet Fuel Reserves, Dutch Government Says
item.description: The Dutch government estimated the European Union has enough kerosene supply to last about five months, drawing on domestic production and strategic reserves amid global energy disruptions.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Indian Rupee Falls as Markets Brace for Ceasefire Expiry
item.description: The Indian rupee logged its steepest one-day fall in a week as wary investors braced for the end of the US-Iran ceasefire amid rising Middle East tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran signals no plans to join US peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
item.description: Iran says it has no plans to send negotiators to Islamabad for a new round of talks with the United States, citing US violations of the ceasefire including the naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. Less than 48 hours remain before the fragile ceasefire expires.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU hosts Gaza peace conference as Orbans defeat opens path for action on Israel
item.description: More than 60 nations sent representatives to Brussels for talks on stability and peace in Gaza and the West Bank. Hungarys election defeat of Israel ally Viktor Orban removes a key veto that had blocked EU sanctions on Israeli settlers and other actions.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Bulgarias Kremlin-friendly ex-president set for landslide election win
item.description: Former president Rumen Radev appears poised for a landslide victory in Bulgarias election, potentially giving pro-Russian forces control of the government in an EU and NATO member state.

item.title: 📺 Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
item.description: Former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgarias general election with a clear majority, the biggest result for a single party since 1997. The pro-Russian leaders victory reshapes Bulgarias political landscape.

item.title: 🇨🇾 Turkeys Fidan slams Greek Cypriots and Israel alliance against Muslim countries
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan criticized Greek Cypriots and Israel for forming an alliance against Muslim countries during a diplomatic visit, highlighting regional tensions amid the broader Middle East crisis.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky questions US envoys Moscow visit while Kushner meeting expected
item.description: President Zelensky criticized US envoys for visiting Moscow without stopping in Kyiv, calling it disrespectful. The US special envoy Witkoff is expected to meet with Kushner as Ukraine policy tensions continue.

item.title: 🇫🇷 French conservatives select Bruno Retailleau as presidential candidate
item.description: French conservative party Les Republicains has selected Bruno Retailleau as their presidential candidate for the upcoming election, consolidating the right-wing opposition to President Macron.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Turkey to train 200 Nigerian special forces under new defence deal
item.description: Turkey will train 200 Nigerian special forces soldiers under a new bilateral defence agreement, expanding Ankaras military partnerships in Africa amid broader regional realignments.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran nuclear talks raise questions about uranium stockpiles
item.description: Questions remain about what will happen to Irans uranium stockpiles as negotiations continue, with experts warning that any deal must address the accumulated nuclear material.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China warships conduct Pacific drills amid Japan tensions
item.description: Chinese warships conducted military exercises in the Pacific as tensions with Japan escalate, with Beijing criticizing Japanese statements about Taiwan.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel entrenches hold on south Lebanon, warns residents to stay out
item.description: Israel has reinforced its positions in southern Lebanon and warned residents to stay away from certain areas, maintaining a security zone despite international calls for withdrawal.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China warns US-Philippines-Japan drills risk regional trust
item.description: China criticized joint military exercises between the US, Philippines and Japan as undermining regional trust, as Beijing continues to oppose expanding security partnerships in the Pacific.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines and US allies begin military exercises testing real-world readiness
item.description: Philippine and US allied forces launched major military exercises focused on real-world operational readiness, amid ongoing tensions with China over disputed territories.

item.title: 📺 Israel-Lebanon peace chances assessed as ceasefire holds
item.description: NPR examines the prospects for lasting peace between Israel and Lebanon as the ceasefire continues to hold, with challenges remaining on both sides of the border.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Bulgaria election: Pro-Russian Radev wins big in EU member state
item.description: Pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgarias election with a strong majority, potentially shifting the EU and NATO member states alignment toward Moscow.
